title: What If the Sky
poem_text:
What if the sky forgets to shine?
And leaves the stars all out of line?
Why does the wind hum lullabies
But never sings them to the skies?
How does a cloud know when to cry?
Or hold its tears and float on by?
Can shadows dance when lights go out?
Or do they just stand there and pout?
What if the dark is just a door
That leads to dreams we’ve seen before?
And if I whisper, soft and low—
Will morning hear the words I know?
cover_context: A child lying on their back in a field at dusk, staring up at swirling clouds that slowly turn into shapes of questions.
style_prompt: Children's musical poem, guitar and gentle beatbox, 112 BPM, dreamy and wondering, spacious, leaves room to think, clear warm vocal, every word easy to understand
